description: Automated Outlook account access with recovery email verification using stealth Chromium

cloakbrowser.description: Stealth Chromium that passes every bot detection test. 57 C++ source-level fingerprint patches. Drop-in Playwright replacement.

launch_persistent_context.description: Launch with persistent profile (cookies/localStorage survive restarts)

microsoft_graph_api.description: Microsoft Graph API for email access (OAuth2 required)
microsoft_graph_api.note: Basic auth disabled since 2024. Requires OAuth2 or App Password.

gmail_imap.description: Gmail IMAP for reading verification codes

outlook_login_flow.description: Automated Outlook login with recovery email verification

substeps: If no code → click 'Use your password' (dispatchEvent)

steps.note: Direct navigation bypasses passkey setup page

key_discoveries.use_password_click: Regular click() fails on span.fui-Link. Must use dispatchEvent with MouseEvent (mousedown/mouseup/click) on the leaf node element.
key_discoveries.passkey_bypass: Passkey setup page has no skip button. Navigate directly to Outlook URL — cookies are already set from password acceptance.
key_discoveries.send_code_required: Must click 'Send code' button before code is sent. Code is NOT sent automatically when entering recovery email.
key_discoveries.recovery_matching: Masked email like 'na*****@gmail.com' can match multiple candidates. Try each one.
key_discoveries.code_timeout: If code not received after 2 min, fall back to 'Use your password' — likely rate limited.

known_issues.imap_code_timeout: Microsoft sometimes doesn't send codes to recovery emails. Rate limiting suspected. Fallback: 'Use your password' works reliably.
known_issues.passkey_setup: No skip button visible. Workaround: navigate directly to Outlook URL.
known_issues.login_slow: ~3 min per account due to 2-min IMAP polling timeout. Optimization: reduce timeout or skip IMAP entirely and always use password fallback.

optimization_ideas: Skip IMAP polling entirely — always use 'Use your password' fallback (saves 2 min per account)
Parallel account processing with multiple browser instances
Session persistence via launch_persistent_context() to skip verification on repeat logins
Proxy rotation for batch processing to avoid rate limits
